trade.summary: The Netherlands is the United States' fifth-largest trading partner globally and its largest in the EU. Netherlands exports chemicals, machinery, pharmaceutical products, and petroleum products; US exports chemicals, machinery, aircraft, and agricultural products. Rotterdam's role as Europe's largest port amplifies the bilateral trade figures as some trade reflects Netherlands' re-export function. ASML's dependency on US technology (and US companies' dependency on ASML) creates deep bilateral supply chain entanglement.
disputes: Ongoing ASML chip equipment export control negotiations
Occasional disputes over tax treaty optimization by US multinationals using Dutch holdings
Trans-Atlantic trade policy disagreements managed through EU-US frameworks

diplomatic.summary: Excellent bilateral relations as longstanding NATO allies and close democratic partners. The US liberated the Netherlands in 1944-45, and this historical bond remains significant. Both countries share deep commitments to NATO, democracy, the rule of law, and the liberal international order. The Netherlands is one of the US's most reliable European allies. The semiconductor technology link (ASML-TSMC-US chip supply chains) has given the bilateral relationship a new critical infrastructure dimension. Under Trump's return, the US pushed aggressively on ASML export restrictions to China, creating some friction but the core alliance remains strong.
recent_incidents: US pressure on Netherlands to restrict ASML chip equipment exports to China
Netherlands aligned with US on Ukraine military aid
Dutch F-35 procurement deepens air force interoperability

military.summary: Very close NATO allies. Netherlands hosts US military assets at Volkel Air Base (B-61 nuclear weapons under NATO sharing), Eindhoven Air Base logistics, and various NATO command elements. Netherlands purchases F-35s from the US, deeply integrating air forces. Intelligence sharing is extensive through Five Eyes-adjacent arrangements. Both countries lead NATO responses to Russian aggression.

financial.summary: Deeply intertwined financial systems. Netherlands is among the largest sources of FDI into the US and vice versa, partly reflecting Dutch holding company structures. Shell, ASML, ING, Unilever, Philips, and Stellantis all have major US operations. US firms (ExxonMobil, Google, Apple) use Dutch holding structures. The bilateral investment relationship is one of the deepest in the world.

energy.summary: US is a growing LNG supplier to Europe via Netherlands. Rotterdam's Gate LNG terminal handles significant US LNG imports. ExxonMobil and Shell (joint Dutch-US heritage) have integrated global energy operations. US shale gas has partially replaced Russian gas in Netherlands.

scientific.summary: Very deep scientific cooperation. ASML-MIT/Stanford/Cornell collaborations on semiconductor physics. NIH funds joint research with Dutch biomedical institutions. The US-Netherlands fulbright program is among the largest. Joint research in climate science, AI, and quantum computing.
